text: This video shows the removal of lymph nodes by the external iliac artery and the obturator fossa.
The surgeon begins by splitting the tissue above the external iliac artery and identifies the vessels.
Here is the artery with the vein below.
The surgeon has started dissecting the nodes along the vessels.
Here is the superior vesicle artery.
It is important to keep lateral to this.
The nodes are carefully removed using scissors and diathermy.
The surgeon then cuts into the obturator fossa and identifies the obturator nerve.
The nodes are situated over the nerve and are carefully dissected.
The nodes are placed in formalin and immediately sent to the pathologist.
Here, most of the nodes are removed and the obturator nerve and superior vesicle artery are visible.
